message.content:
GAPS FOUND: 9

GAP 1
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Payment failure state not properly isolated
What breaks: When a payment fails, it might affect other customers' subscriptions
Real scenario: Customer A's payment fails, but Customer B's active subscription gets incorrectly paused or canceled due to shared state
AI agent prompt: Write a test that creates two separate customers with active subscriptions. Simulate a payment failure for Customer A and verify that Customer B's subscription remains unaffected. Check all subscription states, billing status, and access levels for both customers before and after the payment failure.

GAP 2
Severity: HIGH
Title: Webhook idempotency race condition
What breaks: Duplicate webhook events processed when idempotency check fails under high load
Real scenario: Two identical payment.failed webhooks arrive simultaneously, first creates a failure record but idempotency check fails, second one gets processed and creates duplicate state
AI agent prompt: Write a test that sends two identical payment.failed webhook events simultaneously with the same idempotency key. Verify that only one failure record is created in payment_failures table and that subscription state is updated correctly only once.

GAP 3
Severity: HIGH
Title: Variant limit calculation doesn't handle time zones
What breaks: Overage charges calculated incorrectly when usage crosses limit at timezone boundaries
Real scenario: A customer's usage limit is reset at UTC midnight, but their local time is 5 hours behind, causing them to be charged for 5 hours of usage before the limit actually resets
AI agent prompt: Write a test that creates a customer with a variant limit, tracks their usage across a timezone boundary, and verifies that overage charges are only applied after the actual limit reset time in their timezone, not UTC.

GAP 4
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Subscription state loss during system restart
What breaks: In-memory subscription state not persisted properly on service restart
Real scenario: System restarts during an active subscription modification, leaving subscription in an intermediate state that's not reflected in the database
AI agent prompt: Write a test that creates a subscription, initiates an update (like changing plans), restarts the service during the update, and verifies that the subscription state in the database is consistent and not corrupted.

GAP 5
Severity: HIGH
Title: Missing rollback for partial payment processing
What breaks: When payment processing partially succeeds, completed changes aren't rolled back
Real scenario: A subscription upgrade starts - new plan is activated but proration calculation fails, leaving customer in inconsistent state with no clear path back
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates a partial payment processing failure where the subscription plan was updated but the proration calculation failed. Verify that the system rolls back the subscription to its previous state and logs the failure appropriately.

GAP 6
Severity: MEDIUM
Title: Webhook sequence loss during high load
What breaks: Webhook processing order not maintained under heavy load
Real scenario: Multiple webhooks arrive in quick succession, causing them to be processed out of order, resulting in incorrect subscription states
AI agent prompt: Write a test that sends multiple webhooks (subscription.created, subscription.updated, payment.succeeded) in a specific sequence with timestamps close together. Verify that they are processed in the correct order based on their sequence numbers and timestamps.

GAP 7
Severity: HIGH
Title: Silent failure in PaddleClient retry mechanism
What breaks: Retries fail silently, making it appear like operations succeeded when they didn't
Real scenario: Paddle API is temporarily unavailable, all retries fail, but the client reports success without actual completion
AI agent prompt: Write a test that simulates Paddle API unavailability during a subscription creation attempt. Verify that the retry mechanism exhausts all attempts and properly reports failure rather than silently succeeding.

GAP 8
Severity: CRITICAL
Title: Tenant isolation leak in webhook processing
What breaks: One customer's webhook data accidentally processed for another customer
Real scenario: A webhook for Customer A is incorrectly processed with Customer B's context, leading to Customer B's subscription being modified based on Customer A's data
AI agent prompt: Write a test that processes a webhook for Customer A but accidentally uses Customer B's subscription context. Verify that the system detects this mismatch and rejects the webhook without affecting either customer's actual data.

GAP 9
Severity: HIGH
Title: Proration audit doesn't capture all edge cases
What breaks: Proration calculations not fully audited for complex upgrade/downgrade scenarios
Real scenario: Customer upgrades from Growth to High variant mid-billing cycle, but the proration audit doesn't capture all line items, leading to incorrect billing
AI agent prompt: Write a test that performs a complex subscription upgrade mid-billing cycle with multiple add-ons and discounts. Verify that all proration calculations are captured in the proration_audits table and that the final invoice matches expected amounts.
